project: Better Game — UX Polish, Grading Overhaul, and E2E Tests
description: The game works end-to-end but needs polish in several areas to feel like a finished product. This PRD covers five UX improvements (early mic permission, host audio upload fallback, recording visualizer, loading jokes during grading, and a complete GPT prompt rewrite) plus comprehensive Playwright e2e test coverage. Each improvement is scoped to be implementable in a single focused session.

userStories.title: Request Microphone Permission on Join
userStories.description: As a player joining a game, I want to be prompted for microphone permission when I enter my name so that mic issues surface immediately — not mid-game when it's my turn.
acceptanceCriteria: When a guest player lands on the Join page (/join/{code}), after they enter their name and before or as they submit, the browser's microphone permission prompt is triggered
If permission is granted, store that state so we don't re-prompt later during mic test
If permission is denied, show a friendly warning: "Microphone access was denied. You can still play, but the host may need to upload audio on your behalf."
The mic stream is immediately released after permission is obtained (don't keep it open)
This does not block joining the game — players can still join even if they deny mic access
Existing mic test flow during the play phase still works and skips the permission request if already granted

userStories.title: Host Audio Upload Fallback
userStories.description: As a host, I want to upload an audio file on behalf of a player whose microphone isn't working so that the game doesn't stall.
acceptanceCriteria: On the host's Play page, when a player's turn is active and the player is in the recording or choosing state, the host sees an "Upload Audio for [Player Name]" button
Clicking the button opens a file picker that accepts common audio formats: .mp3, .wav, .webm, .m4a, .ogg
The uploaded file is sent to the existing storeAudio endpoint (or a new host-specific endpoint) with proper authorization
The server converts/stores the audio and triggers the same transcription + grading pipeline as a normal recording
After upload, the turn progresses to grading status identically to a player-recorded turn
Non-host players do not see the upload button
The upload button is only visible during the active player's recording phase, not after audio is already submitted

userStories.title: Audio Visualizer During Recording
userStories.description: As a player recording my explanation, I want to see animated audio bars responding to my voice so I know the microphone is actually capturing audio.
acceptanceCriteria: During the recording phase, the player's phone screen shows an audio visualizer (vertical bars that react to voice volume)
The visualizer uses the existing Web Audio API AnalyserNode setup (already in the codebase for mic test) to get frequency/time domain data
Bars animate smoothly — rising when the player speaks louder, falling during silence
The visualizer is displayed between the topic text and the countdown timer
Minimum 5 bars, styled to match the app's design system (Tailwind classes)
The visualizer only appears on the recording player's phone, not on the host/TV screen
Performance: visualizer runs at ~30fps without janking the recording or countdown timer

userStories.title: Loading Jokes During Grading Wait
userStories.description: As a player waiting for my explanation to be graded, I want to see funny one-liners cycling on screen so the wait doesn't feel like a dead screen.
acceptanceCriteria: After a player's audio is uploaded and the turn enters grading status, the active player's screen shows rotating joke text
Start with a static array of at least 3 generic jokes/one-liners (hardcoded, easily expandable later)
Jokes cycle every 4-5 seconds with a fade or slide transition

Example jokes (placeholder — can be swapped later): "Did you know? Most refrigerators are just cold boxes with anxiety.", "Fun fact: Nobody actually knows how printers work. Not even printers.", "Your explanation is being reviewed by our finest artificial judges..."
The jokes replace the current static "Your explanation is being graded..." text
When grading completes (poll detects grading_complete), jokes stop and navigation to results happens as before
The joke array lives in a dedicated file or constant that's easy for the developer to update later

userStories.title: GPT Grading Prompt Overhaul — Tone and Scoring
userStories.description: As the game designer, I want the GPT grading prompt rewritten so scores are varied and precise, the tone is a dry roast (like a close friend making fun of you), and genuinely good explanations are rewarded with top marks.
acceptanceCriteria: Score precision: Scores use up to one decimal place (e.g., 34.5, 72.8, 91.0). The score field in the Turn model and database supports decimal values (float/decimal column, not integer)

Score distribution: Scores spread across the full 0-100 range. A mediocre rambling explanation might get a 28.5. A decent attempt with some right ideas gets a 55. Near-perfect gets 95+. The prompt explicitly instructs GPT not to cluster around 60-80

Tone — dry roast: The feedback reads like a close friend making fun of you. Dry, not overtly sarcastic. Quotes specific things the player said that were, in retrospect, pretty dumb. Makes them feel dumb but in a friendly, lovable way

Failing grades: GPT is not afraid to give F grades and single-digit scores when the explanation is completely wrong or nonsensical. The prompt explicitly tells GPT that low scores are expected and encouraged when warranted

Rewarding excellence: If a player genuinely nails an explanation with accurate detail, they get a 95-100 and the feedback acknowledges their expertise (can still be funny, but respectful of the achievement)

Grade mapping updated: Grade letters map to the new score range realistically (e.g., A: 90-100, B: 80-89.9, C: 70-79.9, D: 60-69.9, F: below 60) — or whatever mapping makes the most sense for the new distribution
Feedback length: 3-5 sentences, with at least one direct quote from the player's transcript highlighted for comedic effect
actual_explanation: Remains factual and helpful (3-5 sentences), no humor here — this is the "learn something" moment
All existing tests that reference integer scores are updated to handle decimal scores
The Results page displays scores with one decimal place (e.g., "72.5/100")
Player cumulative scores in the scoreboard handle decimal addition correctly

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Smoke and Homepage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright smoke test that verifies the app loads and the homepage renders correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test loads the homepage and verifies it renders without errors
Test verifies key elements are present (app name, navigation, or join link)
Test runs in under 5 seconds

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Host Creates Game
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies a host can create a new game from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as a host user (e.g., user 3 via /dev/login-as/3)
Test navigates to game creation and creates a game
Test verifies redirect to lobby page with a game code displayed
Test verifies the host appears in the player list

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Guest Joins Game via Join Page
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies a guest can join an existing game.
acceptanceCriteria: Test uses a seeded lobby game (e.g., LOBBY1)
Test navigates to /join/LOBBY1, enters a name, and submits
Test verifies redirect to the lobby page
Test verifies the new player appears in the player list or that the lobby page loads correctly

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Host Starts Submission Phase
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the host can transition a game from lobby to submitting.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as the lobby game host (user 3, game LOBBY1)
Test clicks "Start Submission Phase" button
Test verifies redirect to the submit page (/games/LOBBY1/submit)

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Player Submits Topics
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies a player can submit their 3 topics during the submission phase.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as the submitting game host (user 6, game SUBMIT) or joins as a guest player on a submitting game
Test fills in 3 topic fields and submits
Test verifies success feedback or that submission count updates

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Host Starts Game When All Submitted
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the host can start the game when all topics are submitted.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as the ready game host (user 7, game READY1)
Test clicks "Start Game" button
Test verifies redirect to the play page (/games/READY1/play)

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Active Player Chooses Topic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the active player can choose a topic from their options.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as a player in the choosing state (use dev route to log in as the choosing player on game CHOOSE)
Test verifies topic choices are displayed
Test clicks one of the topic choices
Test verifies the turn advances to the recording state (topic is shown, recording UI appears)

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Results Page Displays Correctly
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the results page shows grade, score, feedback, and actual explanation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as the graded game host (user 9, game GRADED)
Test navigates to the results page for the completed turn
Test verifies the grade badge, score, feedback text, and actual explanation are all visible
Test verifies the scoreboard shows all players with scores

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Host Advances Turn from Results
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the host can advance to the next player from the results page.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as the graded game host (user 9, game GRADED)
Test navigates to results and clicks "Next Player" button
Test verifies navigation to the play page for the next turn

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Round Complete and Next Round
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the round complete page shows the scoreboard and the host can start the next round.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as the round-done host (user 10, game RNDDNE)
Test verifies the round complete page shows scores for all players
Test clicks "Start Next Round" (if multi-round) or "Finalize" button
Test verifies navigation to the next game state

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Completed Game Final Results
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the completed game page shows final rankings and all turn results.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as the veteran host (user 5, game COMPLT)
Test navigates to /games/COMPLT/complete
Test verifies final rankings are displayed with player names and scores
Test verifies individual turn results (topics, grades) are shown
Test verifies a "Play Again" button is present

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Guest Reconnection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies a guest player can reconnect to a game after refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as a guest player on a seeded game via /dev/login-as-player/{playerId}
Test verifies the correct game page loads
Test refreshes the page
Test verifies the player is still in the game and on the correct page (not kicked back to join)

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — Host Dashboard Shows Active Games
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies the host dashboard shows their active games with rejoin links.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as a host with active games (e.g., user 2 who hosts game PLAYNG)
Test navigates to /dashboard
Test verifies at least one game is listed with its code and status
Test clicks "Rejoin" and verifies redirect to the correct game page

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test — State Redirect Middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright test that verifies players are redirected to the correct page when they try to access a wrong game phase.
acceptanceCriteria: Test logs in as a host for a game in playing status
Test manually navigates to /games/{code}/lobby (wrong phase)
Test verifies automatic redirect to the correct page (/games/{code}/play)
